status_detail: Green hydrogen production at PKN Orlen's Plock refinery, Poland. 5 MW alkaline electrolysis. Operational since 2021. Producing green hydrogen for refinery operations. Part of Orlen's broader hydrogen strategy targeting 1 GW of electrolysis capacity by 2030.

data_management.narrative_summary: PKN Orlen has deployed green hydrogen production at its Plock refinery in Poland, starting with a 5 MW alkaline electrolysis plant commissioned in 2021. The plant produces green hydrogen for the refinery's hydrotreating and desulfurization operations. Orlen is Poland's largest energy company and is developing a multi-GW hydrogen pipeline as part of its strategy to reach 1 GW of installed electrolysis capacity by 2030. The Plock project provides operational experience with alkaline electrolysis in a refinery environment and serves as the foundation for Orlen's larger planned hydrogen projects in Poland and the Baltic region.

sources.title: PKN Orlen — hydrogen strategy and Plock electrolysis project
sources.author: PKN Orlen SA
